name: Santa Maria de Trassierra — circular from Villaviciosa de Cordoba
name_original: Santa María de Trassierra — circular desde Villaviciosa de Córdoba
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villaviciosa De Córdoba features a landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense Mediterranean forests, and significant elevation changes. The region is part of the Sierra Morena mountain range, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Rivers and reservoirs, such as the Puente Nuevo, are prominent features, providing scenic routes. This area presents a mix of challenging climbs and winding paths through natural woodlands. Best touring cycling routes around Villaviciosa De Córdoba The most popular touring cycling route is Santa Maria de Trassierra — circular from Villaviciosa de Cordoba, a 50.8 miles (81.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation changes and panoramic views across the landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop, a difficult 36.0 miles (57.9 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Camino del Bejarano – Arroyo Bejarano loop from Cerro Muriano, a 55.9 miles (90.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain including hills and arroyos, often completed in about 8 hours. Touring cycling around Villaviciosa De Córdoba is defined by rolling hills, dense forests, and scenic reservoir paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to challenging cycling routes. The routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 290 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villaviciosa De Córdoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Villaviciosa De Córdoba, offering a diverse range of experiences from moderate to challenging. The region is known for its rolling hills and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: While many routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba feature significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. Most routes tend to be moderate to difficult, so beginners should check route profiles carefully.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring bike trails in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: The terrain in Villaviciosa De Córdoba is characterized by rolling hills, dense Mediterranean forests, and significant elevation changes, as it's part of the Sierra Morena mountain range. You'll encounter a mix of challenging climbs and winding paths through natural woodlands.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027281277/santa-maria-de-trassierra-circular-from-villaviciosa-de-cordoba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de Trassierra — circular from Villaviciosa de Cordoba&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 81.7 km.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33883246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Bejarano – Arroyo Bejarano loop from Cerro Muriano&lt;/a&gt; offer varied terrain and significant climbs over 90 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027156501/puente-nuevo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 57.9 km long with substantial elevation.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region offers several natural highlights. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/624215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Popea Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, various springs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4700544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;First Spring of the Bejarano Stream&lt;/a&gt;, and scenic lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5083196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake La Encantada&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, several routes feature the region's prominent rivers and reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027156501/puente-nuevo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Nuevo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadanuño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5832119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: The touring cycling routes in Villaviciosa De Córdoba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and the scenic beauty of the Mediterranean forests and reservoirs.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or monuments to look out for?
A: Beyond natural features, you might come across interesting landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5292457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Fountain – Arroyo Bejarano loop from El Puerto&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features various springs, some of which are considered natural monuments.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Villaviciosa De Córdoba?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer. However, the varied terrain means routes can be enjoyed year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are there any routes that start near Córdoba?
A: Yes, some routes are accessible from nearby areas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33912709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcubilla Spring – Los Villares Campsite loop from Córdoba&lt;/a&gt; is a 35.8 km route that starts from Córdoba itself, offering a convenient option for those based in the city.

This route is very popular in the summer because its numerous shadows make it possible to train in the hot afternoons of southern Spain, as the thermometers touch the 40º C temperature.

This bridge crosses the Guadiato river, a beautiful place in the middle of Sierra Morena, this road takes us to the town of Villaviciosa de Córdoba or to the Trassiera neighborhood through dirt tracks, perfect for our gravel routes.
